
  ---
  ANUBIS V2 — SENIOR PRINCIPAL ENGINEER CODEBASE REVIEW

  ---
  A. Executive Summary

  Overall Codebase Maturity Score: 3.5 / 10

  This is a structurally aware scaffold with good bones but pre-production gaps that would cause serious problems if
  deployed today. The architectural decisions are thoughtful — monorepo, route groups, design token system, schema-first
   API design — but the implementation is incomplete in ways that matter: auth doesn't enforce multi-tenancy, the data
  layer is a flat JSON file with no concurrency protection, critical settings live in localStorage, and there are zero
  automated tests. A 30-engineer team would not accept a single PR in this state without 6–8 blocking comments.

  That said: this is not a badly designed codebase. It is an under-built one. The difference matters. The skeleton is
  right. The organs haven't been installed yet.

  ---
  Top 5 Biggest Risks

  ┌─────┬──────────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  #  │                 Risk                 │                            If Not Fixed                             │
  ├─────┼──────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 1   │ Auth middleware exists but does not  │ Any authenticated user can read, edit, or delete any other user's   │
  │     │ scope data by user                   │ opportunities                                                       │
  ├─────┼──────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 2   │ JSON file as primary data store      │ Concurrent writes corrupt data; server restart loses in-flight      │
  │     │                                      │ writes; no rollback; no queries                                     │
  ├─────┼──────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 3   │ Settings stored in localStorage      │ Multi-device breaks; private session data in browser storage;       │
  │     │                                      │ backend AI calls use client-injected prompts                        │
  ├─────┼──────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 4   │ No rate limiting on AI endpoint      │ Single user can exhaust your Anthropic API budget in minutes; no    │
  │     │                                      │ cost control                                                        │
  ├─────┼──────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 5   │ No error tracking or structured      │ Production failures are invisible; debugging requires SSH into the  │
  │     │ logging                              │ server                                                              │
  └─────┴──────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  ---
  Top 5 Highest-Impact Improvements

  ┌─────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  #  │                   Improvement                    │                         Payoff                         │
  ├─────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 1   │ Migrate JSON → Supabase (migration SQL already   │ Real persistence, multi-tenancy, RLS, concurrency      │
  │     │ written)                                         │                                                        │
  ├─────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 2   │ Enforce user_id filtering in all backend routes  │ Actual data isolation; fixes the auth bypass           │
  ├─────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 3   │ Move settings to Supabase user_settings table    │ Multi-device, server-validated AI context, no          │
  │     │                                                  │ localStorage trust                                     │
  ├─────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 4   │ Add rate limiting + token cap to                 │ Cost protection; prevent abuse                         │
  │     │ /generate-job-insights                           │                                                        │
  ├─────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ 5   │ Add GitHub Actions CI (lint + tsc + test)        │ Prevents regressions from landing; forces test culture │
  └─────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  ---
  B. Critical Issues

  ---
  Issue 1: Auth Middleware Exists But Doesn't Enforce Multi-Tenancy

  Why it matters: Every authenticated user can call GET /jobs and receive all opportunities in the JSON file. There is
  no user_id scoping. Once real users log in, they can read, update, or delete each other's data. This is a data breach,
   not a bug.

  Where: backend/server.js — requireAuth middleware extracts req.user but it is never referenced in readJobs(), GET
  /jobs, GET /jobs/:id, PUT /jobs/:id, or DELETE /jobs/:id. The comment in the code explicitly acknowledges this: "TODO:
   filter by user_id once Supabase migration is complete."

  Recommended fix:
  // All read/write operations must be scoped:
  const jobs = readJobs().filter(j => j.user_id === req.user.id);
  // And on create, always stamp user_id:
  newJob.user_id = req.user.id;
  This is not optional post-Supabase. It must be wired before any real user can sign up.

  Priority: CRITICAL

  ---
  Issue 2: JSON File Is Not a Production Data Store

  Why it matters: backend/data/jobs.json is a flat file read-write with no locking. Two simultaneous POST requests
  trigger two readJobs() calls, both get the same array, both write back — the second write silently overwrites the
  first. On Render or Railway, every deploy wipes the file. There are no transactions, no foreign keys, no query
  capability.

  Where: backend/server.js — readJobs() / writeJobs() are the entire data layer. Used in every route handler.

  Recommended fix: The migration SQL is already written at backend/migrations/001_opportunities.sql. This is the work,
  not the design. Execute it:
  // Replace readJobs() / writeJobs() with:
  const { data, error } = await supabase
    .from('opportunities')
    .select('*')
    .eq('user_id', req.user.id);
  Every route handler needs this swap. The schema is already correct.

  Priority: CRITICAL

  ---
  Issue 3: Settings Stored in localStorage — Client Injects AI System Prompts

  Why it matters: frontend/app/(app)/settings/page.tsx stores all contractor configuration including base_prompt,
  business_context, system_prompt, and tone in localStorage under the key anubis_global_settings. The opportunity detail
   page reads these from localStorage and sends them to the backend inside the request body:

  // From opportunity/[id]/page.tsx (inferred from audit):
  body: {
    ...jobFields,
    system_prompt: settings.base_prompt,   // from localStorage
    business_context: settings.business_context  // from localStorage
  }

  The backend then injects these verbatim into the Claude API call. This means:
  1. A user can craft any system prompt they want and send it to your AI endpoint
  2. Settings don't persist across devices or browsers
  3. Settings are wiped on localStorage.clear() or private browsing
  4. There is no server-side validation of what gets inserted into Claude's system prompt (prompt injection vector)

  Where: frontend/app/(app)/settings/page.tsx (localStorage writes), backend/server.js POST /generate-job-insights
  (reads and uses system_prompt from body)

  Recommended fix:
  1. Create a user_settings table in Supabase
  2. Save settings via PUT /settings API (authenticated, validated with Zod)
  3. On AI generation, fetch settings server-side by req.user.id — never trust the client to provide a system prompt
  4. Sanitize/cap the system prompt length (e.g., 2,000 chars max) and validate it doesn't contain injection patterns

  Priority: CRITICAL

  ---
  Issue 4: No Rate Limiting on AI Endpoint

  Why it matters: POST /generate-job-insights calls claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 with a max of 1024 tokens. There is no
  rate limiting, no per-user quotas, no daily caps. A single malicious or buggy client can loop this endpoint and cost
  you hundreds of dollars in minutes.

  Where: backend/server.js — POST /generate-job-insights, backend/ai-provider.js

  Recommended fix:
  npm install express-rate-limit
  const rateLimit = require('express-rate-limit');
  const aiLimiter = rateLimit({
    windowMs: 60 * 1000,  // 1 minute
    max: 10,              // 10 AI calls per minute per IP
    message: { error: 'Too many AI requests, slow down' }
  });
  app.post('/generate-job-insights', requireAuth, aiLimiter, validate(generateInsightsSchema), ...);
  Also add per-user daily limits tracked in Supabase (or Redis in Phase 2).

  Priority: CRITICAL

  ---
  Issue 5: No Request Size Limits — DoS Vector

  Why it matters: Express 5 by default parses JSON bodies without a size cap. A client can POST a 50MB JSON body to any
  route, causing the server to OOM or exhaust memory. This is a trivial DoS.

  Where: backend/server.js — app.use(express.json()) with no options

  Recommended fix:
  app.use(express.json({ limit: '50kb' }));
  app.use(express.urlencoded({ extended: false, limit: '50kb' }));

  Priority: CRITICAL

  ---
  Issue 6: Supabase SDK Version Mismatch

  Why it matters: Backend uses @supabase/supabase-js@2.104.1, frontend uses @supabase/supabase-js@2.49.4. These are 55
  minor versions apart. Session token format changes, auth API changes, and RLS behavior changes between minor versions.
   The frontend is dramatically older. When you implement Supabase auth, the JWT format and session object shape from
  the frontend client may not match what the backend SDK expects.

  Where: backend/package.json vs frontend/package.json

  Recommended fix: Align both to the same version. Given the backend is newer, update the frontend:
  cd frontend && npm install @supabase/supabase-js@2.104.1
  Then test session handling end-to-end.

  Priority: HIGH

  ---
  Issue 7: Express 5 in Production

  Why it matters: Express 5 (^5.2.1) was released as stable in September 2024 but the ecosystem (middleware, tutorials,
  Stack Overflow answers) is still almost entirely Express 4. More importantly, Express 5 changes async error
  propagation — unhandled promise rejections in route handlers now auto-propagate to error middleware, which is good,
  but if you have any async route handlers that don't explicitly try/catch, their errors will silently fall through
  unless you have a proper global error handler. Currently, backend/server.js has no global error handler middleware
  (app.use((err, req, res, next) => ...)).

  Where: backend/package.json, backend/server.js

  Recommended fix: Add a global error handler at the bottom of server.js:
  app.use((err, req, res, next) => {
    console.error('[ERROR]', err.stack);
    res.status(err.status || 500).json({
      error: err.message || 'Internal server error',
      ...(process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development' && { stack: err.stack })
    });
  });
  This alone will fix silent 500s in production.

  Priority: HIGH

  ---
  Issue 8: Prices Stored as Strings

  Why it matters: price, bid, payments_received, and balance_due are stored as string | number in the Zod schema and as
  TEXT in the SQL migration (implied by the schema and normalizeJob handling). The balance_due field is used for risk
  scoring logic in opportunity/[id]/page.tsx. Comparing text representations of money ("2400" > "500" is false in string
   comparison) will produce wrong risk scores.

  Where: backend/schemas.js — jobUpsertSchema uses z.union([z.string(), z.number()]) for price fields;
  backend/migrations/001_opportunities.sql — likely TEXT columns; frontend/app/(app)/opportunity/[id]/page.tsx — risk
  score computation

  Recommended fix:
  1. In the SQL migration, change price columns to NUMERIC(12,2)
  2. In jobUpsertSchema, coerce to number: z.coerce.number().nonnegative()
  3. Always serialize as number in normalizeJob()
  4. In frontend risk scoring, use parseFloat() before comparisons (or remove the need with proper typing)

  Priority: HIGH

  ---
  Issue 9: customer_name AND first_name/last_name — Redundant Fields

  Why it matters: The data model has both customer_name (full name string) and first_name + last_name (structured). The
  normalizeJob() function in server.js derives customer_name from first_name + last_name if the stored value is empty.
  This means the same conceptual field has two representations and they can drift out of sync. AI prompts use
  customer_name for personalization but the form collects first_name/last_name. A job created via API with only
  first_name will show correctly in the app but fail AI personalization if customer_name isn't recomputed.

  Similarly, address and address_1 are duplicate fields.

  Where: backend/server.js — normalizeJob(), backend/data/jobs.json, backend/migrations/001_opportunities.sql

  Recommended fix:
  1. Drop customer_name — derive it always from first_name + ' ' + last_name in normalizeJob()
  2. Drop address — use only the structured fields (address_1, city, state, zip_code)
  3. Add a full_address computed getter in normalizeJob() for display
  4. Update the SQL migration to remove the redundant columns before running it in production

  Priority: HIGH

  ---
  Issue 10: Opportunity ID Prefix Logic Is Fragile and Confusing

  Why it matters: Draft opportunities have an opportunity_id prefixed with "P". When first saved (promoted from Draft),
  server.js strips the "P" prefix. This logic is buried in the update handler with no clear comment explaining the
  convention. It also means the ID format changes on first save, breaking any external references (bookmarks, shared
  links, API calls) made while the record was a Draft. The ID format YYMMDDXXXX also doesn't guarantee uniqueness when
  two records are created in the same second.

  Where: backend/server.js — ID generation and Draft promotion logic

  Recommended fix:
  1. Use a UUID for the internal ID (auto-generated by Postgres/Supabase)
  2. Use a separate, immutable display_id column generated once on creation: YYYYMMDD-NNNN (sequential per user per day)
  3. Remove the "P" prefix hack entirely — Draft status is tracked by the status field, not the ID
  4. The SQL migration already has id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY — use it

  Priority: HIGH

  ---
  Issue 11: No Error Tracking or Structured Logging

  Why it matters: In production, errors are invisible. console.log statements in server.js go to Render/Railway log
  streams but are unstructured, have no correlation IDs, and are never aggregated. There is no Sentry, no Datadog, no
  structured JSON logging. When the AI call fails at 2am, you will not know until a user complains.

  Where: Entire backend — backend/server.js, backend/ai-provider.js

  Recommended fix:
  npm install pino pino-pretty  # structured JSON logger
  npm install @sentry/node  # error tracking
  Add a correlation ID to every request (crypto.randomUUID()) and log it with every error. Sentry captures stack traces
  automatically. This takes 2 hours and pays dividends forever.

  Priority: HIGH

  ---
  Issue 12: Frontend Uses React Hook Form Dependency But Opportunity Form Uses Manual State

  Why it matters: frontend/package.json includes react-hook-form@7.60.0 as a dependency, but the 500-line
  opportunity/[id]/page.tsx manages form state manually with useState for each field. This means: no validation on blur,
   no dirty-field tracking, no form-level isSubmitting guard, no field-level error display, and a re-render on every
  keystroke as state updates cascade. For a 25+ field form, this is a performance problem and a UX problem.

  Where: frontend/app/(app)/opportunity/[id]/page.tsx, frontend/package.json

  Recommended fix: Either use react-hook-form (which you're already paying for in bundle size) or remove the dependency.
   Don't ship unused dependencies. If using it:
  const { register, handleSubmit, formState: { errors, isDirty, isSubmitting } } = useForm<OpportunityFormData>({
    resolver: zodResolver(opportunitySchema),
    defaultValues: job
  });
  This also enables proper unsaved-changes warnings (isDirty check before navigation).

  Priority: HIGH

  ---
  Issue 13: No CSRF Protection

  Why it matters: The backend uses Authorization: Bearer <token> headers, which provides CSRF immunity for AJAX requests
   (browsers don't auto-attach Authorization headers). However, if any future route accepts cookies for auth (which
  Supabase can do in SSR mode), all mutation routes become CSRF-vulnerable without explicit protection. This is a
  design-time decision that's easier to get right now.

  Where: backend/server.js — CORS config, auth middleware

  Recommended fix: Enforce Content-Type: application/json on all mutation routes and reject requests without it. Never
  add cookie-based auth without a CSRF token mechanism. Document this as a constraint in CLAUDE.md.

  Priority: MEDIUM

  ---
  Issue 14: Admin Login Page Exists But Is Not Functional or Guarded

  Why it matters: frontend/app/(marketing)/admin-login/page.tsx is a public route with no implementation. Any search
  engine crawler, security scanner, or curious user will find /admin-login. It signals an admin interface exists,
  provides an attack surface, and may be confusing to users who stumble across it.

  Where: frontend/app/(marketing)/admin-login/page.tsx

  Recommended fix: Either move it behind the (app) auth guard (as /admin/login) and implement it properly, or delete it
  until Phase 2. Don't ship placeholder admin routes on public paths.

  Priority: MEDIUM

  ---
  C. Architecture Recommendations

  What Must Change Structurally

  1. Split backend/server.js into layers. At 376 lines it's already too large, and it will grow to 1,000+ lines as Phase
   2 adds email, SMS, Stripe, proposals, and calendar. A monolithic server file is fine for a weekend project. It is not
   fine for a 30-engineer team.

  Recommended backend structure:
  backend/
  ├── server.js              # App bootstrap ONLY (express init, middleware, import routes, listen)
  ├── middleware/
  │   ├── auth.js            # requireAuth, optionalAuth
  │   ├── validate.js        # Zod validation wrapper
  │   └── rateLimit.js       # Per-route rate limiters
  ├── routes/
  │   ├── health.js          # GET /, GET /health
  │   ├── opportunities.js   # /jobs CRUD
  │   ├── actions.js         # /jobs/:id/action
  │   ├── ai.js              # /generate-job-insights
  │   └── settings.js        # /settings (Phase 2)
  ├── services/
  │   ├── opportunityService.js   # Business logic (normalizeJob, stale detection, ID gen)
  │   ├── aiService.js            # AI orchestration (prompt building, response parsing)
  │   └── settingsService.js      # User settings CRUD
  ├── db/
  │   ├── supabase.js        # Supabase client singleton
  │   └── queries/
  │       ├── opportunities.js
  │       └── settings.js
  ├── config/
  │   └── env.js             # (already exists, keep)
  ├── schemas/
  │   └── index.js           # (rename schemas.js → schemas/index.js, add settings schema)
  └── migrations/
      └── 001_opportunities.sql

  2. Migrate settings to server-side. user_settings table in Supabase, fetched via authenticated API. Remove
  localStorage dependency for anything security-relevant.

  3. Consolidate the anubisv2/ folder. The root directory has both the AnubisV2 project directory and the anubis-alpha/
  monorepo. The reviewer/executor Python framework in Claude/ is a separate tool, not part of the product. Move it out
  or .gitignore it.

  4. Add TypeScript to the backend. The frontend is strict TypeScript. The backend is untyped CommonJS JavaScript. This
  creates a documentation gap: the API contract exists in schemas.js (Zod) but has no TypeScript types. When a frontend
  developer wants to know what a Job looks like, they read the schema — but there's no shared type definition.

  Recommended approach: Convert backend to TypeScript (or at minimum add JSDoc types). Share Zod schemas as a
  packages/shared workspace. Both frontend and backend import from the same source of truth.

  5. Add a shared types package:
  anubis-alpha/
  ├── packages/
  │   └── shared/
  │       ├── schemas.ts    # Zod schemas (shared)
  │       └── types.ts      # Inferred TypeScript types
  ├── backend/
  └── frontend/
  Both backend/ and frontend/ import from @anubis/shared. This eliminates the current situation where schemas.js
  (backend) and any validation in the frontend must be kept in sync manually.

  ---
  D. File-by-File Review

  ---
  backend/server.js (376 lines)

  What it does: Single-file Express application — CORS, auth middleware, JSON file I/O, all route handlers, business
  logic (stale detection, ID generation, job normalization), AI call orchestration.

  What is good:
  - CORS allowlist (not wildcard)
  - requireAuth middleware exists and is applied to mutation routes
  - normalizeJob() gives a consistent API response shape
  - Stale/aged detection logic is readable

  What should change:
  - Split into routes/, services/, middleware/ (see Architecture section)
  - Add global error handler (app.use((err, req, res, next) => ...))
  - Add express.json({ limit: '50kb' }) size cap
  - Replace readJobs()/writeJobs() with Supabase queries
  - Add per-route rate limiting on the AI endpoint
  - normalizeJob() should live in services/opportunityService.js
  - Remove customer_name redundancy (derive from first/last)
  - Remove address redundancy (derive from structured fields)
  - The "P" prefix Draft promotion logic must be deleted after migrating to Supabase UUIDs

  Specific edit — add global error handler (line 376+):
  // Must be last middleware, after all routes
  app.use((err, req, res, next) => {
    const status = err.status || err.statusCode || 500;
    const message = status < 500 ? err.message : 'Internal server error';
    console.error(`[${new Date().toISOString()}] ERROR ${status}:`, err);
    res.status(status).json({ error: message });
  });

  ---
  backend/ai-provider.js (47 lines)

  What it does: Thin Claude SDK wrapper — initializes the Anthropic client lazily, calls messages.create, extracts text
  blocks.

  What is good:
  - Singleton pattern (lazy init)
  - Clean error message if API key missing
  - Minimal, single-responsibility

  What should change:
  - Model ID is read from env.anthropicModel — good. But maxTokens: 1024 is hardcoded here instead of configurable. For
  longer proposals, this will silently truncate responses.
  - No retry logic. If Anthropic returns a 529 (overloaded) or 500, the error propagates to the user. Add exponential
  backoff for transient errors.
  - No timeout. A hung Anthropic request will hang the Express response indefinitely.

  Specific edit:
  const response = await client.messages.create({
    model: env.anthropicModel,
    max_tokens: env.anthropicMaxTokens || 1024,  // make configurable
    messages: [{ role: 'user', content: prompt }]
  }, {
    timeout: 30000  // 30s hard timeout
  });

  ---
  backend/schemas.js (79 lines)

  What it does: Zod schemas for request validation — jobUpsertSchema, actionSchema, generateInsightsSchema, and
  validate() middleware.

  What is good:
  - validate() middleware pattern is clean and reusable
  - jobUpsertSchema uses .trim() and .default('') for robustness
  - generateInsightsSchema uses .passthrough() correctly

  What should change:
  - Price fields should be z.coerce.number().nonnegative().optional() not z.union([z.string(), z.number()]). The union
  type will accept "abc" as a valid price if the string branch matches.
  - actionSchema.type should export its enum values as a const for reuse in route handlers (avoid magic strings in
  server.js)
  - Add a settingsSchema now (even if the route is Phase 2) — document the shape before building it

  ---
  backend/src/config/env.js (115 lines)

  What it does: Loads env vars, parses types, reports service status.

  What is good:
  - Falls back to .env.example with a warning (good for local dev)
  - getServiceStatus() pattern is excellent for health checks
  - assertServiceConfigured() is a good guard for Phase 2 routes

  What should change:
  - The fallback to .env.example if .env is missing is dangerous in production. It will silently use example values
  (empty strings) instead of crashing loudly. Add a NODE_ENV === 'production' check that exits immediately if .env is
  missing:
  if (process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production' && !fs.existsSync(envPath)) {
    console.error('FATAL: .env file required in production');
    process.exit(1);
  }
  - Path: backend/src/config/env.js — this is the only file in backend/src/. Either move env.js to backend/config/env.js
   or create the full src/ structure. A lone file in a deep path is confusing.

  ---
  backend/migrations/001_opportunities.sql (140 lines)

  What it does: Supabase Postgres schema for the opportunities table with RLS policies, indexes, and triggers.

  What is good:
  - RLS policies covering all 4 operations (select/insert/update/delete)
  - Auto-updated updated_at trigger
  - CHECK constraints on milestone and status enums
  - user_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users ON DELETE CASCADE — correct FK

  What should change:
  - Price columns: verify they are NUMERIC(12,2) not TEXT. If they're TEXT, fix before running this migration.
  - Add a settings table migration in 002_user_settings.sql now, while the data model is still being designed.
  - Add an index on (user_id, milestone) and (user_id, status) for the filtered queries the app will make most
  frequently.
  - Missing: opportunity_id should have a UNIQUE constraint per user: UNIQUE (user_id, opportunity_id)
  - Missing: email and phone are free text with no format validation. Add CHECK constraints or handle at application
  layer. (At minimum, note this as a known gap.)

  ---
  frontend/lib/api.ts (94 lines)

  What it does: API client — auth header injection, response error handling, typed fetch wrappers.

  What is good:
  - Throws at module load if NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL is missing — prevents silent routing to wrong endpoint
  - authHeaders() always attaches a Bearer token — consistent auth
  - handleResponse() parses error body in a standard format
  - All functions throw on error — no hidden error objects

  What should change:
  - authHeaders() calls supabase.auth.getSession() on every request. This is async and hits localStorage on every API
  call. Cache the session in a module-level variable and invalidate on onAuthStateChange. For a page with 5 simultaneous
   API calls on mount, this fires 5 separate session reads.
  - There is no global loading/error state management. Each page manages its own isLoading / error state with useState.
  This means loading spinners, error toasts, and retry logic are copy-pasted across every page component. Consider React
   Query or SWR as a caching layer.
  - apiUpload sets no Content-Type header (correct for multipart), but there's no file size validation client-side
  before upload. Add a size check (e.g., 10MB) before calling this function.

  ---
  frontend/lib/auth.ts (32 lines)

  What it does: useSession() hook + signOut() function.

  What is good:
  - Subscribes to onAuthStateChange for real-time session updates
  - Returns 'loading' state as a first-class value (prevents auth flicker)
  - Clean, minimal

  What should change:
  - useSession() creates a new Supabase subscription on every component that calls it. If AppShell and (app)/layout.tsx
  both call useSession(), you have two separate subscriptions. Move the session to a React Context (AuthContext) so the
  subscription is created once and all components read from the same state.
  - signOut() doesn't redirect after sign out. The caller must handle navigation. This is fine but should be documented.

  ---
  frontend/app/(app)/layout.tsx (36 lines)

  What it does: Client-side auth guard — redirects to /login if no session, shows spinner if loading, wraps children in
  AppShell.

  What is good:
  - Clean three-state handling (loading / null / session object)
  - Uses router.replace (not push) — correct for auth redirects

  What should change:
  - This is a client component doing auth gating, which means the page HTML is rendered on the server without auth, then
   the client checks auth and redirects. This causes a flash of the page content (or a blank screen) before the redirect
   fires.
  - Better approach: Use Next.js middleware (middleware.ts at the project root) to redirect unauthenticated requests
  server-side before any page renders. Supabase has first-class support for this with @supabase/ssr.

  ---
  frontend/app/(app)/opportunity/[id]/page.tsx (~500 lines)

  What it does: Opportunity detail form — load/create/edit/delete job, risk scoring, NBA computation, AI insight
  generation.

  What is good:
  - Risk scoring and NBA logic are co-located with the form (acceptable at this scale)
  - AI response parsing (split on ---UPSELL---) is clearly defined

  What should change:
  - 500 lines in a single component is too large. Extract at minimum: <OpportunityForm />, <RiskScorePanel />,
  <AIInsightsPanel />, <ContactActionsBar />
  - Manual useState for 25+ fields — use react-hook-form (already in package.json)
  - No unsaved-changes guard — navigating away silently discards edits. Add isDirty check + beforeunload warning.
  - "Duplicate" functionality — currently creates a new record with the same fields. There's no visual confirmation or
  success redirect. The UX is undefined behavior.
  - Risk scoring is frontend-only — if accessed via API directly, a client would receive no risk score. Move to
  normalizeJob() in the backend so all consumers get it.
  - NBA computation duplicates backend flag logic — the backend already computes isStale / isAged. The frontend
  re-derives NBA from raw fields. These should share a single decision function.

  ---
  frontend/app/(app)/settings/page.tsx (~300 lines)

  What it does: Global settings form — saves everything to localStorage.

  What is good:
  - Covers a comprehensive set of configuration options
  - Settings are logically grouped (Company, AI, Email, Defaults, etc.)

  What should change:
  - localStorage is wrong for production settings — migrate to Supabase (see Critical Issue #3)
  - The settings page renders a massive single component. Extract each settings group into its own component:
  <CompanySettings />, <AISettings />, <EmailSettings />, etc.
  - There is no validation on save. A user can set max_sms_length to -1 or stale_lead_days to "banana" and it persists
  silently.
  - There is no success/failure toast on save (or if there is, it's not triggered by a server response — it's always
  success because localStorage never fails).

  ---
  frontend/app/(app)/dashboard/page.tsx (286 lines)

  What it does: Analytics dashboard — loads all jobs, computes revenue metrics, renders Recharts bar charts.

  What is good:
  - Recharts ResponsiveContainer is used correctly (fills parent width)
  - Metric cards are computed client-side from the jobs array (appropriate for Phase 1 scale)

  What should change:
  - Loads ALL jobs on every dashboard visit — as the dataset grows to hundreds of opportunities, this is a full table
  scan on every render. Add server-side aggregation endpoints (e.g., GET /stats/summary) that return pre-computed
  metrics.
  - Revenue metric naming is unclear: "open revenue", "lead revenue", "completed revenue" — these should be defined in a
   constants file and match the terminology in the SQL schema.
  - No loading skeleton — the page shows nothing while fetching. Add skeleton cards (shadcn Skeleton component is
  already installed) so the layout doesn't jump.
  - Charts have no empty state — if jobs is [], the charts render empty axes. Add an empty state: "No opportunities yet.
   Create your first one."
  - No error state — if apiGet('/jobs') throws, the dashboard silently shows zeros. Add an error banner.

  ---
  frontend/components/app-shell.tsx (~100 lines)

  What it does: Left sidebar + top bar navigation.

  What is good:
  - usePathname() for active route highlighting
  - Mobile-responsive (sidebar hidden on small screens)
  - "Alpha build" info card communicates pre-production status clearly

  What should change:
  - Search input is non-functional — it's a placeholder input that does nothing. Either wire it to a global search or
  remove it. A non-functional UI element erodes trust.
  - Notifications bell is non-functional — same issue. Remove until Phase 2.
  - Avatar/user menu is a placeholder — at minimum, clicking the avatar should show a dropdown with "Sign out".
  Currently it does nothing.
  - Nav items for Calendar (Soon), Outreach (Soon), Uploads (Soon) are visible but disabled. This is fine for an alpha,
  but the disabled state should be visually distinct (muted text + lock icon) and not look like broken links.

  ---
  frontend/lib/supabase.ts (11 lines)

  What it does: Initializes the Supabase browser client.

  What is good:
  - persistSession: true and detectSessionInUrl: true are correct for magic link auth

  What should change:
  - The fallback placeholders ('placeholder' for the Supabase URL/key) mean the client initializes without error even in
   a misconfigured environment. This is hiding misconfiguration. Throw instead:
  if (!process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL) {
    throw new Error('NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL is required');
  }
  - This file should not be used server-side. The server/supabase.ts (with service role key) exists for that. Add a
  comment and an import 'client-only' guard.

  ---
  frontend/app/(marketing)/login/page.tsx

  What it does: Magic link login page with a dev bypass button.

  What is good:
  - Dev bypass button is clearly marked — acceptable for Phase 1

  What should change:
  - Magic link form is a placeholder — the actual supabase.auth.signInWithOtp() call must be implemented before any real
   user can log in. This is the top critical path item.
  - No error state — if the magic link fails (invalid email, Supabase error), the user sees nothing.
  - No rate limiting on the client — users can spam the magic link button. Disable the button for 60 seconds after
  submission.
  - admin-login page is publicly accessible — see Critical Issue #14.

  ---
  E. Refactor Roadmap

  Phase 1: Must Fix Before Any Real User Touches This

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────┬────────┐
  │                            Task                             │                 File(s)                  │ Effort │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Implement Supabase magic-link auth in /login                │ login/page.tsx, lib/auth.ts              │ 4h     │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Migrate data layer from JSON file to Supabase               │ server.js → db/queries/opportunities.js  │ 8h     │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Enforce user_id scoping on all backend queries              │ server.js (all route handlers)           │ 4h     │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Move settings from localStorage to Supabase user_settings   │ settings/page.tsx, new                   │ 8h     │
  │ table                                                       │ routes/settings.js                       │        │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Add global error handler to Express                         │ server.js (bottom of file)               │ 1h     │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Add request size limits                                     │ server.js                                │ 30min  │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Add rate limiting to /generate-job-insights                 │ server.js, new middleware/rateLimit.js   │ 2h     │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Fix Supabase SDK version mismatch                           │ frontend/package.json                    │ 30min  │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Remove customer_name / address redundancy                   │ server.js, migration SQL                 │ 3h     │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Fix price fields to numeric type                            │ schemas.js, migration SQL,               │ 3h     │
  │                                                             │ normalizeJob()                           │        │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Delete or hide admin-login page                             │ (marketing)/admin-login/page.tsx         │ 30min  │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Add Next.js middleware auth guard (replace client-side      │ frontend/middleware.ts                   │ 3h     │
  │ redirect)                                                   │                                          │        │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Add server-only guard to lib/supabase.ts                    │ lib/supabase.ts                          │ 30min  │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Wire avatar menu to sign out                                │ components/app-shell.tsx                 │ 1h     │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Delete template debris from /public                         │ frontend/public/logos/, public/avatars/  │ 30min  │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────┴────────┘

  ---
  Phase 2: Should Fix Within First Two Weeks of Launch

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┬────────┐
  │                                Task                                 │             File(s)              │ Effort │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Split server.js into routes/, services/, middleware/                │ Full backend refactor            │ 12h    │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Add Pino structured logging to backend                              │ All route handlers               │ 4h     │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Add Sentry error tracking (frontend + backend)                      │ server.js, layout.tsx            │ 3h     │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Convert opportunity form to react-hook-form                         │ opportunity/[id]/page.tsx        │ 8h     │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Extract <RiskScorePanel />, <AIInsightsPanel />, <ContactActionsBar │ opportunity/[id]/page.tsx        │ 6h     │
  │  />                                                                 │                                  │        │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Move risk scoring to backend normalizeJob()                         │ server.js                        │ 3h     │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Add server-side aggregation endpoint GET /stats                     │ New route                        │ 4h     │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Add loading skeletons to dashboard and jobs list                    │ dashboard/page.tsx,              │ 3h     │
  │                                                                     │ jobs/page.tsx                    │        │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Add empty states and error states to all data-driven pages          │ All (app) pages                  │ 4h     │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Add GitHub Actions CI (lint + tsc + test)                           │ .github/workflows/ci.yml         │ 3h     │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Add first integration tests (opportunity CRUD)                      │ backend/__tests__/               │ 8h     │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Create shared types package (packages/shared)                       │ Monorepo                         │ 6h     │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Add AuthContext to replace useSession() per-component               │ frontend/lib/auth.ts             │ 4h     │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Remove or wire search/notifications in app-shell.tsx                │ components/app-shell.tsx         │ 2h     │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Validate settings on save (Zod)                                     │ settings/page.tsx                │ 3h     │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Add unsaved-changes guard to opportunity form                       │ opportunity/[id]/page.tsx        │ 2h     │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Fix AI max_tokens to be configurable                                │ ai-provider.js, config/env.js    │ 1h     │
  ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
  │ Add retry logic to AI provider                                      │ ai-provider.js                   │ 2h     │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────┴────────┘

  ---
  Phase 3: Future-Proofing (Before Scaling)

  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                    Task                    │                               Payoff                               │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Convert backend to TypeScript              │ Type-safe API, shared schema enforcement                           │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Add React Query / SWR to frontend          │ Caching, background refresh, optimistic updates, deduped requests  │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Add E2E tests with Playwright              │ Catch regressions across full auth + CRUD flows                    │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Add feature flags (PostHog or              │ Ship partial features safely                                       │
  │ LaunchDarkly)                              │                                                                    │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Add audit log table (opportunity_events)   │ Compliance, debugging, changelog UI                                │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Add per-user AI usage tracking             │ Cost attribution, billing signals, abuse prevention                │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Build admin dashboard                      │ User management, system health, feature flag management            │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Add OpenTelemetry tracing                  │ Distributed tracing across frontend → backend → Supabase →         │
  │                                            │ Anthropic                                                          │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Extract AI prompts to a prompt registry    │ Version prompts, A/B test them, roll back bad prompts              │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Add Stripe for billing                     │ Revenue, subscription management, usage-based billing              │
  └────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  ---
  F. Final Recommendation

  Is This Launch-Ready?

  No. Not to real users with real data.

  The structural choices are correct and the product vision is clear, but three blockers make this unshippable to a
  paying customer today:

  1. Any authenticated user can read any other user's data. This is not a future-proofing concern — it's a privacy
  breach. It must be fixed before user #2 signs up.
  2. The data store is a JSON file that disappears on every deploy. A user will save an opportunity, you'll deploy a
  fix, and their data will be gone. This will happen within the first week.
  3. The magic link auth flow is not implemented. Users cannot actually log in. The "dev bypass" works locally but has
  no place in production.

  What Would You Fix First?

  In strict priority order:
  1. Implement Supabase auth (magic link, real session, JWT validation)
  2. Migrate to Supabase Postgres (the migration SQL is already written — this is execution, not design)
  3. Add user_id filtering to all queries (one line per route handler, enormous security impact)
  4. Move settings server-side (direct attack vector if left in localStorage)
  5. Add the global error handler (30 minutes, stops silent 500s)

  Steps 1–3 together take roughly two focused engineering days. After that, the product has a real data layer, real
  auth, and real isolation. Everything else is polish and scale.

  What Would You Refuse to Ship?

  - The current backend without the global error handler and request size limits — silent failures in production are
  unacceptable
  - Any route that reads or writes without user_id scoping — this is a data breach waiting to happen
  - The admin-login public route — remove it or guard it
  - The localStorage settings feeding AI system prompts — prompt injection surface that bypasses all your Zod validation

  What Is Acceptable Technical Debt?

  - CommonJS backend — fine for now, convert to TypeScript in Phase 3
  - No test suite — painful but survivable at this stage; add CI and integration tests in Phase 2
  - Manual form state in the opportunity form — ugly but functional; react-hook-form migration is Phase 2
  - No structured logging — survivable for early users, add Pino + Sentry before 100 users
  - Stub pages (calendar, outreach, uploads) — clearly marked as coming soon, no harm
  - react-hook-form in package.json but not used — fix in Phase 2 when you wire the form; acceptable for now
  - Express 5 — not ideal for production readiness, but with a proper error handler in place it's manageable

  Blunt Summary

  This codebase reads like it was built by someone who knows how to design systems but ran out of time finishing them.
  The architecture decisions are defensible, the design tokens are thoughtful, the migration SQL is already written, and
   the docs are surprisingly good. But none of that matters if a user can read another user's opportunities, if a deploy
   wipes the data, or if an AI endpoint has no cost guardrails.

  Fix auth, fix the data layer, fix multi-tenancy scoping. Everything else is a better product — those three are a
  working one.
